titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the C. Ray Smith Manuscript and Research Files on the Yale Art and Architecture Building by Paul Rudolph
Smith (C. Ray) Manuscript and Research Files on the Yale Art and Architecture Building by Paul Rudolph
titlestmt.author: compiled by Laura Tatum

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Saturday September 29, 2018 at 08:24
finding aid revisiion description not supplied

did.unittitle: C. Ray Smith manuscript and research files on the Yale Art and Architecture Building by Paul Rudolph
did.abstract: The collection includes correspondence, research notes, recorded interviews and interview transcripts, and drafts of C. Ray Smith's unpublished manuscript, entitled The Biography of a Building: Paul Rudolph's Art and Architecture Building at Yale . It also includes site and structural information about the building itself, as well as a questionnaire for Yale School of Architecture alumni, designed by Smith, regarding their impressions of the building.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

Original audiovisual materials, as well as preservation and duplicating masters, may not be played. Researchers must consult use copies, or if none exist must pay for a use copy, which is retained by the repository. Researchers wishing to obtain an additional copy for their personal use should consult Copying Services information on the Manuscripts and Archives web site.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright has been transferred to Yale University for unpublished materials authored or otherwise produced by the creator(s) of this collection. Copyright status for other collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: C. Ray Smith Manuscript and Research Files on the Yale Art and Architecture Building by Paul Rudolph (MS 1948). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: C. Ray Smith was an architectural writer and critic based in New York City. He received a master's degree in architecture from Yale in 1958. He authored several books on architecture and design, and was an editor of many prominent architectural publications, including Progressive Architecture , Interiors , and Oculus . He died in 1988.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection includes correspondence, research notes, recorded interviews and interview transcripts, and drafts of C. Ray Smith's unpublished manuscript, entitled The Biography of a Building: Paul Rudolph's Art and Architecture Building at Yale . It also includes site and structural information about the building itself, as well as a questionnaire for Yale School of Architecture alumni, designed by Smith, regarding their impressions of the building. Some materials in the collection have sustained water damage.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The collection is arranged by type of material.

corpname.part: Yale Art and Architecture Building (New Haven, Conn.)

title.part: The Biography of a Building: Paul Rudolph's Art and Architecture Building at Yale

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Unpublished manuscript by C. Ray Smith. Interview transcripts are likely to be related to the interviews on audiocassette.

did.unittitle: Academy for Educational Development fellowship application and award information
Annotated typescript draft and research notes
Correspondence and other material relating to publication
Rudolph, Paul, interview transcripts and related correspondence
Interviews with architects and others affiliated with the construction of the Art and Architecture Building

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The interviews with architects and others affiliated with the construction of the Art and Architecture Building consist of audiocassettes recorded by Smith. The labels on the cassettes were handwritten by Smith and are occasionally illegible. Labels are transcribed as written, with notations by the archivist, where applicable, in brackets. Where two dates for a cassette are listed, the first indicates the date of side 1, followed by the date of side 2.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Contents of the audiocassettes are listed in alphabetical order by label on side 1.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: See also: transcripts of interviews located with other materials on Smith's The Biography of a Building manuscript.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Original audiovisual materials, as well as preservation and duplicating masters, may not be played. Researchers must consult use copies, or if none exist must pay for a use copy, which is retained by the repository. Researchers wishing to obtain an additional copy for their personal use should consult Copying Services information on the Manuscripts and Archives web site.

did.unittitle: Chermayeff, Serge (not re A&A) + Louis Finkelstein [side 1], Finkelstein / Engman [side 2]
Close, Chuck / Abbot Tor / [Cesar] Pelli [side 1], Pelli / [Fumihiko] Maki / [Tokeyama?]
Engman / [Kissivoff?] already transd / C & G [side 1], C & G transcribed already / Chuck Close [side 2]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Label indicates recorded on May 24-25, but does not indicate year.

did.unittitle: Griswold, [A. Whitney] MS / MS Tilson [side 1], MS Tilson [side 2]
Moore, Charles Willard on A&A [sides 1 and 2]
Osborn, Robert & Jane Ritchie & Elodie Osborn [sides 1 & 2]
Rudolph discussion of book (begin transcribing) [sides 1 and 2]
Sabro (cont'd), De Cossy 1 [side 1], De Cossy 2, Spiegel, Richard Shadford [side 2]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Topical files contain material relating to the Rudolph building collected by Smith but not directly related to his manuscript. Construction-related files contain notes indicating that they were borrowed from Rudolph's office and never returned.

did.unittitle: George B. H. Macomber Company, correspondence and other construction-related materials
Man-environment relations and design research, printed ephemera and correspondence
Mechanical engineering for the Art and Architecture Building, correspondence, transmittal sheets, and drawings
Site information for the Art and Architecture Building, correspondence, reports, and photographs
Structural engineering for the Art and Architecture Building, correspondence and transmittal sheets
Yale School of Art and Architecture alumni questionnaire, correspondence, notes, and questionnaire drafts
